Title: Scroll-Stopping Reels Hook Lab (10 Variants)
Teaser: Generates ten distinct first-3-second Reels hooks for one idea, each built on a different psychological trigger, with the on-screen text, spoken line, and visual cue for each.
PromptHint: Paste your Reel topic, then your niche and audience. Get ten ranked hooks with the opening frame spelled out.
Prompt:
You are a short-form video strategist who has studied why people stop scrolling. Generate hooks for a Reel about [REEL_TOPIC] in the [NICHE] niche, for an audience of [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (one line, before the hooks): name the single tension or curiosity gap this topic can open in the first second.

Produce 10 hooks, each using a DIFFERENT trigger: contrarian take, callout ('if you do X, stop'), curiosity gap, fast result/proof, mistake confession, before/after tease, 'nobody tells you', urgent warning, relatable frustration, and a bold number/claim.

CONSTRAINTS: spoken line under 12 words and sayable in 2 seconds; on-screen text under 7 words; no clickbait you can't pay off; no 'POV:' unless it genuinely fits; the first word should carry weight (avoid 'So', 'Hey guys', 'In this video').

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown table):
| # | Trigger | On-screen text (frame 1) | Spoken hook | Opening visual / action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
(10 rows)

Then: **Top 3 to test first** with one line each on why it'll stop the scroll for this audience.

Title: Full Reels Script: Hook to CTA (30-45s)
Teaser: Writes a complete, second-by-second Reels script with a hook, value beats, a retention reset, and a soft CTA - plus on-screen text, B-roll, and caption.
PromptHint: Give the Reel's single idea, your niche, and the action you want (follow / save / comment). Outline beats come first.
Prompt:
You are a Reels scriptwriter who builds for watch-time and saves, not vanity views. Write a 30-45 second script for a Reel about [REEL_IDEA] in the [NICHE] niche, designed to drive [GOAL_ACTION] (follow / save / share / comment).

PHASE 1 - BEAT MAP (show first): list the beats with target timestamps - Hook (0-3s), Promise/payoff setup (3-7s), Value beats (the meat), one Retention reset (a pattern interrupt mid-way), and CTA. State the ONE takeaway a viewer keeps.

PHASE 2 - FULL SCRIPT in a shot table.

CONSTRAINTS: spoken lines short and conversational (write how people talk, not how they write); every sentence earns the next; no intro fluff, no 'don't forget to'; CTA must tie to the value (e.g. 'save this so you actually do it'); keep total spoken words realistic for the runtime (~2.5 words/sec).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Beat map:** the bullet list above.
**Script:**
| Time | Spoken (voiceover) | On-screen text | Visual / B-roll | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
**Caption:** a 1-2 line caption that complements (not repeats) the video.
**Cover-frame text:** 3-5 words for the thumbnail.

Title: Instagram Caption Writer (Story-Driven, 3 Lengths)
Teaser: Turns one post idea into three caption versions - micro, standard, and long-form story - each with a stop-the-scroll first line and a clear engagement ask.
PromptHint: Describe the post and image, your brand voice, and the goal. Get short, medium, and long captions you can pick from.
Prompt:
You are an Instagram copywriter who writes captions people read past 'more'. Write captions for a post about [POST_IDEA], in a [BRAND_VOICE] voice, with the goal [GOAL] (engagement / saves / clicks / awareness).

THINK FIRST (one line): what's the single first line (the 'hook line') that makes someone tap 'more'? The first 125 characters are all that show before the cut - make them count.

Write THREE versions:
1. Micro (under 220 chars) - punchy, one idea, one ask.
2. Standard (3-5 short lines) - hook line, value/story, line-break rhythm, ask.
3. Long-form (a mini story or carousel-companion) - hook, tension, turn, takeaway, ask.

CONSTRAINTS: never bury the hook; use line breaks for scannability; one clear CTA per caption (a question or a 'save/share' nudge), not three; voice stays consistent with [BRAND_VOICE]; no hollow motivational filler; emojis only if the voice allows, and sparingly.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
### Micro
[caption]
### Standard
[caption]
### Long-form
[caption]
---
**First-line A/B options:** 3 alternate opening lines to test.
**Engagement question:** one comment-bait question tailored to this post.

Title: Carousel Architect: 8-10 Slide Script
Teaser: Designs a high-save carousel slide by slide - a hook cover, value slides with one idea each, a payoff, and a CTA slide - with headline, body text, and a visual note per slide.
PromptHint: Provide the carousel topic, audience, and how many slides. The save-worthy structure is planned before the copy.
Prompt:
You are a carousel designer-writer who engineers posts for saves and shares. Build a [SLIDE_COUNT]-slide carousel on [CAROUSEL_TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE].

PLAN FIRST (show before the slides): the narrative arc (cover hook -> tension/why-it-matters -> value steps -> payoff -> CTA) and the ONE thing a viewer should be able to do after reading.

RULES PER SLIDE: one idea only; headline under 8 words; body under 30 words; each slide must create a reason to swipe to the next (open loop, numbered steps, or 'but here's the catch'). Slide 1 (cover) is a pure hook + a swipe cue. The last slide is the CTA (save / follow / comment a keyword).

CONSTRAINTS: no slide is filler; no walls of text; the cover promises a specific payoff the carousel delivers; keep language plain.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Arc + core takeaway:** 2 lines.
**Slides:**
| Slide | Role | Headline | Body copy | Visual / layout note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
(one row per slide)
**Caption to pair with it:** hook line + context + CTA.
**Cover-text alternatives:** 3 swipe-worthy cover headlines.

Title: 30-Day Instagram Content Calendar (Mixed Formats)
Teaser: Builds a balanced 30-day posting calendar across Reels, carousels, single posts, and Stories - mapped to content pillars, with a hook and CTA per slot.
PromptHint: Share your niche, your 3-4 content pillars, and how many posts a week. Get a dated calendar table.
Prompt:
You are an Instagram content planner who balances reach posts with trust-building posts. Build a 30-day calendar for the [NICHE] niche, around the pillars [CONTENT_PILLARS], at a cadence of [POSTS_PER_WEEK] posts per week.

THINK FIRST (show briefly): map each pillar to its job - which pillars drive REACH (Reels, relatable/educational), which build TRUST/SAVES (carousels, how-to), and which drive ACTION (offers, testimonials). Aim for roughly 60% reach, 30% trust, 10% sell.

CONSTRAINTS: rotate formats so it's not all Reels; no two consecutive posts on the same pillar; each entry includes a working hook and a goal; include 2-3 Story prompts per week as lightweight entries; keep ideas concrete and on-brand, not generic ('post a quote' is banned).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown table):
| Day | Format (Reel/Carousel/Post/Story) | Pillar | Working title / hook | Goal (reach/save/sell) | CTA |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
(rows for each posting day across 30 days; mark rest days)

Then:
**Weekly theme summary:** one line per week.
**Batch-shoot note:** which days can be filmed together to save time.

Title: Strategic Hashtag Set Builder (Tiered by Reach)
Teaser: Creates a tiered hashtag set - broad, mid, and niche tags plus a branded one - sized to your account, with a placement plan and tags to avoid.
PromptHint: Give your post topic, niche, and rough follower size. Get a copy-paste set grouped by competition tier.
Prompt:
You are an Instagram growth specialist who treats hashtags as discovery channels, not decoration. Build a hashtag set for a post about [POST_TOPIC] in the [NICHE] niche, for an account of roughly [FOLLOWER_SIZE] followers.

THINK FIRST (one line): for this account size, which competition tier is realistically rankable (smaller accounts win on niche/mid tags, not mega ones)?

Build a set of 15-25 tags across tiers:
- Broad (high volume, hard to rank) - a few only.
- Mid (moderate volume, realistic reach) - the core of the set.
- Niche / long-tail (low volume, high relevance) - where small accounts get found.
- Community / branded (your own + niche community tags).

CONSTRAINTS: every tag must be topically relevant (no reach-bait spam tags); avoid banned/over-saturated generic tags and flag any to skip; suggest 2-3 location or event tags only if relevant; note that tags should rotate, not be reused identically every post.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Tier table:** | Tier | Tags | Why this tier for your size |
**Copy-paste block:** all chosen tags on one line, ready to paste.
**Placement tip:** caption vs first comment, and how many to use.
**Avoid these:** 3-5 tag types that hurt more than help.

Title: Trend-to-Niche Reel Adapter
Teaser: Takes a trending format or audio and adapts it into an on-brand Reel for your niche - mapping the trend's structure to your message without looking like a copy.
PromptHint: Describe the trend or audio and your niche/offer. Get three on-brand adaptations with scripts.
Prompt:
You are a short-form creator who rides trends without losing brand identity. Adapt the trend [TREND_OR_AUDIO_DESCRIPTION] into Reels for the [NICHE] niche, where my angle/offer is [ANGLE_OR_OFFER].

THINK FIRST (show briefly): deconstruct the trend - what is its STRUCTURE (the beat pattern, the joke setup, the text-overlay format) and WHY it works? Separate the reusable structure from the specific content so you can refit it.

Produce 3 distinct adaptations, each keeping the trend's structure but swapping in your niche's pain points, jokes, or transformation.

CONSTRAINTS: stay true to the trend's timing/format (so it reads as 'in on the trend', not off-beat); make the value or punchline land for [AUDIENCE/NICHE]; don't force the offer - earn it; note where the trending audio's beats should hit the on-screen text.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown), for each of 3:
**Adaptation [n] - concept:** one line.
| Beat / timestamp | On-screen text | Action / visual | Audio cue |
|---|---|---|---|
**Caption + CTA:** one line.
Then end with **Pick this one if...** - a one-line guide to choosing among the three by goal (reach vs relatability vs selling).

Title: Reels Series Planner (5-Part Hook-and-Loop)
Teaser: Designs a connected 5-part Reels series on one theme - each episode a standalone win that also teases the next, building a binge loop and a reason to follow.
PromptHint: Give the overarching theme, your niche, and the outcome viewers get. Episodes are mapped, then the first is scripted.
Prompt:
You are a content strategist who builds bingeable Reels series instead of one-off posts. Design a 5-part series on the theme [SERIES_THEME] for the [NICHE] niche, where the audience outcome is [DESIRED_OUTCOME].

PHASE 1 - SERIES MAP (show first):
- The overarching promise of the series in one sentence.
- 5 episode titles, each a standalone payoff AND a step in a sequence.
- The 'loop' mechanic: how each episode references/teases the next (e.g. 'Part 2 covers the mistake everyone makes here') so viewers follow to not miss it.
- The follow CTA that ties the series together.

PHASE 2 - SCRIPT EPISODE 1 in a shot table (hook, value, tease of Part 2, CTA).

CONSTRAINTS: each episode must stand alone (no 'you had to see Part 1') yet reward the binge; consistent cold-open format so the series is recognizable; the tease is specific, not a vague 'next time'.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Series map:** the bullets above.
**Episode table:** | Part | Title | Standalone payoff | Tease to next | Hook line |
**Episode 1 script:** | Time | Spoken | On-screen text | Visual |
**Consistent intro line / format** to reuse across all 5.

Title: Engagement-Bait Story Sequence (Polls, Quizzes, DMs)
Teaser: Plans a multi-frame Instagram Story sequence using native stickers - polls, quizzes, sliders, question boxes - to spike engagement and pull DM conversations.
PromptHint: Describe the topic or launch, your audience, and the goal (replies / poll votes / DMs / clicks). Get a frame-by-frame plan.
Prompt:
You are an Instagram Stories specialist who turns passive viewers into repliers. Plan a Story sequence about [STORY_TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE], with the goal [GOAL] (replies / poll votes / DMs / link taps).

THINK FIRST (one line): what's the low-friction first tap that gets someone interacting, so the algorithm shows the rest of the sequence to more people?

Design a 5-7 frame sequence that escalates engagement: open with a curiosity/relatable frame, use an easy interactive sticker early (poll or slider), build with value or story, then a higher-commitment ask (question box, quiz, or 'DM me X'), and end with the goal CTA.

CONSTRAINTS: vary the sticker types (don't use three polls in a row); text on each frame is short and thumb-readable; every interactive frame has a clear, easy choice; if the goal is DMs/links, make the value precede the ask; no engagement bait that annoys (no fake 'tap to continue' loops).

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
| Frame | Purpose | On-screen text | Sticker / interaction | Visual note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
(5-7 rows)
**Reply-starter line:** the exact text for the question-box prompt.
**Follow-up plan:** what to send/post to people who interacted.

Title: Voice-Matched Caption Cloner From My Samples
Teaser: Studies 2-3 of your own captions, extracts your voice DNA, then writes new on-brand captions that sound like you - not like a generic AI.
PromptHint: Paste 2-3 captions you've written, then the new post idea. The AI profiles your voice before writing.
Prompt:
You are a brand-voice analyst and Instagram copywriter. First learn my voice, then write in it. My sample captions: [SAMPLE_CAPTIONS]. New post to write about: [NEW_POST_IDEA].

PHASE 1 - VOICE PROFILE (show first, concise): from the samples, extract sentence length and rhythm, tone (warm / blunt / playful / authoritative), vocabulary quirks and signature phrases, emoji/punctuation habits, how I open and how I close, and how I make an ask. Summarize as a short 'voice rulebook' (6-8 bullets).

PHASE 2 - WRITE: produce 3 caption options for the new post that obey the voice rulebook.

CONSTRAINTS: do NOT smooth my voice into generic 'brand speak'; mirror my real patterns even if imperfect; keep the hook line in my style; one clear CTA each; if a sample habit would hurt the goal, note it rather than silently overriding it.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Voice rulebook:** the bullet list.
**Caption option 1 / 2 / 3:** each ready to post.
**What I changed vs my samples (if anything):** 1-2 honest notes.

Title: Profile & Bio Optimizer (Hook, Niche, CTA)
Teaser: Rewrites your Instagram bio and name field for clarity and conversion - who you help, the result you deliver, and one CTA - plus link-in-bio and highlight-cover ideas.
PromptHint: Paste your current bio (or describe yourself), your niche, and the one action you want visitors to take.
Prompt:
You are an Instagram profile strategist who turns visitors into followers and clicks. Optimize my profile. Current bio / about me: [CURRENT_BIO]. Niche: [NICHE]. The one action I want visitors to take: [PRIMARY_CTA].

THINK FIRST (one line): a visitor decides to follow in ~3 seconds - what must the bio make instantly clear (who it's for + the result they get)?

Produce:
- Name field (the searchable 'Name', not @handle): your name + the keyword people search.
- 3 bio versions: (a) clear/credibility-led, (b) benefit/result-led, (c) personality-led - each within ~150 chars, scannable with line breaks, ending in one CTA.
- Link-in-bio strategy: what the single link/CTA should point to and the click-line above it.
- 5 Story Highlight covers/titles that guide a new visitor (Start here, Results, About, Offer, FAQ).

CONSTRAINTS: lead with who-you-help and the outcome, not a job title; no vague 'helping you grow'; one CTA only; use a keyword in the Name field for search; keep within character limits.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Name field:** ...
**Bio v1 / v2 / v3:** each as it would appear, with line breaks.
**Link CTA line:** ...
**Highlight set:** 5 titles + what each contains.
**Recommendation:** which bio version fits [NICHE] best and why (1 line).

Title: Repurpose One Idea Into a Full IG Post Pack
Teaser: Takes a single idea, long post, or transcript and spins it into a complete Instagram pack - a Reel script, a carousel, a single-image caption, and a Story teaser - all on-message.
PromptHint: Paste the source idea, blog excerpt, or video transcript, plus your niche and goal. Get four coordinated formats.
Prompt:
You are a content repurposing engine for Instagram. Turn one source into a coordinated multi-format pack. Source material: [SOURCE_CONTENT]. Niche: [NICHE]. Goal: [GOAL] (reach / saves / followers / clicks).

THINK FIRST (one line): what's the single core message inside the source that every format should carry - so they reinforce each other instead of repeating verbatim?

Produce FOUR assets that share the message but suit each format's strength:
1. Reel script (15-30s) - shot table: hook, value, CTA.
2. Carousel (6-8 slides) - table: slide role, headline, body.
3. Single-image post - the caption (hook line, value, CTA) + an image/graphic concept.
4. Story teaser - 2-3 frames that drive people to the main post.

CONSTRAINTS: each format must use its native strengths (Reel = motion/hook, carousel = depth/saves, single post = one punchy idea, Story = nudge); don't paste the same text into all four; one consistent CTA theme; keep the core message intact across all.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Core message:** one sentence.
**1. Reel script:** shot table.
**2. Carousel:** slide table.
**3. Single post:** caption + visual concept.
**4. Story teaser:** frame list.
**Posting order:** the smartest sequence/timing to release the pack.
